channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Meet three Nigerian students competing with over 150 countries at global STEM Olympiad
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Three Nigerian students are representing the country at the International STEM Olympiad in Rome, showcasing private support for gifted education.
Read More: https://punchng.com/meet-three-nigerian-students-competing-with-over-150-countries-at-global-stem-olympiad/

item.title: England battle Mexico as Brazil face Norway in last-16 clashes
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
England face Mexico in a highly anticipated World Cup last-16 match at Estadio Azteca. Can the Three Lions overcome altitude and a strong Mexico side?

item.description: All the action from day five of Wimbledon where stars such as Iga Swiatek, Taylor Fritz and Frances Tiafoe were on display. Credit: AELTC.

item.title: West African Female Parliamentarians Unite Against Political Marginalisation

item.description: Female Parliamentarians, under the auspices of the ECOWAS Female Parliamentarians Association (ECOFEPA), have intensified efforts to address the factors militating against female participation in politics and policy-making in the region, following poor female representation in political decision-mak

item.title: FROM HERBS TO ASHES: How Ibadan herb traders lost millions of Naira to market inferno
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: TWENTY-FOUR hours after mysterious fire razed the famous herbs market at Bode, in Molete area of Ibadan, Oyo State, the usual aroma of the dried roots, medicinal leaves, tree barks, and ancient herbal remedies has been replaced by the acrid smell of smoke and ashes. This once-bustling market, where

item.title: World Cup: Mbappé sends France into last eight to face Morocco
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: France will face Morocco in the quarter-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after both nations secured victories in their Round of 16 encounters. The post World Cup: Mbappé sends France into last eight to face Morocco appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

The Nigerian international is one of the players attracting attention this summer after spending the last two seasons w

item.description: “Operations are currently ongoing to rescue those children abducted in Oyo State, and we are making tremendous progress,” the army chief said.

item.description: “We knew nothing about blood group compatibility before we got married in 2012. My wife was said to be B-positive, and I was O-positive as well... item.title: Sam Larry hospitalised, bouncer dies in Lagos road crash

item.description: Nigerian music promoter and socialite, Sam Larry, has reportedly been hospitalised following a road accident along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road in Lagos State. item.description:

item.title: How fake agency ‘DG’ Adeyemi outsmarted federal officials to secure CBN accounts, office space
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Relying on letters from Mr Adeyemi, the OSGF and OAGF processed requests for an office space and CBN accounts for his non-existent agency. item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG

The move is an important step for the 20-year-old, who impressed during his loan spell at Goodison Park and is also attracti

item.title: Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70, PM Modi pays tribute
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Widely regarded as the foremost exponent of Pandavani, Chhattisgarh's traditional folk storytelling form based on episodes from the Mahabharata, Teejan Bai was known for her commanding voice, dramatic stage presence and expressive performances.

item.title: Ekiti Poll: Low Voter Turnout, BVAS Glitches Raise Fresh Concerns Ahead Of 2027 Elections
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Voters in Ekiti trekked to polling units on Saturday only to meet dead BVAS machines and empty queues. The low turnout and tech failures have reignited fears that 2027 may repeat 2023’s logistical problems, civil society groups warned, write ADEMU IDAKWO Low voter turnout, isolated BVAS failur

item.title: China frees jailed pastor Ezra Jin after Trump’s intervention
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Ezra Jin is the founder of Zion Church, one of China's unregistered churches that some Christians choose to worship at instead of state-sanctioned ones regulated by the government. The post China frees jailed pastor Ezra Jin after Trump’s intervention appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: BOK Warns of Risks From Single-Stock Leveraged ETFs: Yonhap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Bank of Korea warned that single-stock leveraged exchange-traded funds tied to Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. could deepen market concentration, amplify volatility and intensify one-way trading flows, according to a local report.

item.title: World Cup 2026: We’ll Do Everything To Bring More Joy To Moroccans –Ounahi
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Morocco star Azzedine Ounahi has disclosed that the Atlas Lions’ biggest reward is seeing the Moroccan people celebrate, emphasizing that the players are determined to do everything to bring more joy to their country. He made these remarks after leading Morocco to a 3-0 victory over Canada in

item.title: Anambra agency destroys ₦50m illicit drugs, arrests suspects
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anambra State Special Anti-Touting Squad (SASA) destroyed ₦50m worth of illicit drugs and arrested suspects in Awka, reaffirming its zero-tolerance stance
Read More: https://punchng.com/anambra-agency-destroys-%e2%82%a650m-illicit-drugs-arrests-suspects/

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Sunday
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers from across the country. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Sunday appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Trump blasts 'communists', celebrates the US in 250th anniversary speech
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Saturday hailed America as the "crowning achievement of human history", in his speech celebrating 250 years of US independence in Washington. Trump claimed that under his presidency the United States was "prouder than ever before, even as he used the event to renew his a

item.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco Coach Ouahbi Talks Up Win Over Canada
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Morocco head coach Mohamed Ouahbi has reacted to his team’s victory over Canada, Completesports.com reports. The Atlas Lions thrashed the co-hosts 3-0 in Houston to earn a place in the quarter-finals of FIFA World Cup 2026. Canada dominated the opening half, but second-half braces from Azzedin

item.title: Sam Larry hospitalised as fatal Lagos crash kills bouncer
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigerian music promoter and socialite, Sam Larry, has reportedly been hospitalised after he was involved in a fatal road accident along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road in Lagos State. The post Sam Larry hospitalised as fatal Lagos crash kills bouncer appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Every Look Nana Akua Addo Wore to Celebrate Ghana at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Ghanaian fashionista Nana Akua Addo debuted three custom outfits celebrating the Black Stars during their 2026 World Cup run, translating matches against England, Croatia, and Colombia into bold national flag-inspired style statements. The post Every Look Nana Akua Addo Wore to Celebrate Ghana at th

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mexico Coach Shrugs Off Altitude Factor Against England
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Mexico’ coach Javier Aguirre has dismissed concerns and discussions about the altitude at Estadio Azteca being an advantage for his team ahead of their FIFA World Cup 2026 round-of-16 clash against England. Aguirre stated that the physical condition will not decide the game, noting, “It’

item.title: Democracy cannot thrive when money remains determinant of electoral success —Bolanle Sarumi
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: In this interview with Yetunde Ajanaku, politician and former governorship candidate, Bolanle Aminat Sarumi-Aliyu, reflects on the defining moments of her political journey, the setbacks that tested her resolve, and why she remains committed to public service despite repeated disappointments. YOU ha

item.title: APC expands Senate lead to 88 seats despite defections
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The All Progressives Congress, APC, has tightened its grip on the Senate after pushing its membership to 88 lawmakers, following the inauguration of four new senators last Tuesday. The post APC expands Senate lead to 88 seats despite defections appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Fewer Ships Transiting Hormuz Along Oman Coast After U-Turns
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The number of vessels sailing through the Strait of Hormuz along the Omani coast fell to a trickle on Sunday, a day after several exiting along that route made sharp reversals, underscoring Iran’s continued efforts to maintain its grip on the waterway.

item.title: World Cup: Mbappe penalty sends France into quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: France booked their place in the quarter-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after Kylian Mbappe's second-half penalty secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory over a resilient Paraguay in Philadelphia on Saturday. The post World Cup: Mbappe penalty sends France into quarter-finals appeared first on The Sun

item.title: Beeg float library, a Rust port of Fabrice Bellard's libbf
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/lifthrasiir/libbeef Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48791440 Points: 14 # Comments: 3

item.title: My ICAN journey started at 13 – Nigeria’s youngest chartered accountant
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria's youngest chartered accountant, Osasere Okundaye, has revealed how her journey with the Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (ICAN) began. The post My ICAN journey started at 13 – Nigeria’s youngest chartered accountant appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Modi’s Biofuel Push Comes Under Pressure as Motorists Protest
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indian motorists will gather in New Delhi on Sunday, in the first major protest against a flagship biofuel program intended to reduce crude imports and support farm incomes.

item.title: Trump lauds Americans in storm-delayed 250th anniversary speech
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: President Trump has praised Americans as ‘a historic and heroic people’, in a speech on the nation's 250th anniversary.

item.title: Gbajabiamila on the crucifix
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE late Chief Joseph Folahan (JF) Odunjo left behind an allegory that perfectly mirrors today’s most notorious national scandal. It explains the infamy surrounding President Bola Tinubu’s Chief of Staff, Femi Gbajabiamila and allegedly self-appointed DG of Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotio

item.title: Mouse: Precision Editing Tools for AI Coding Agents
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
